data.title: Genesis Minerals Price Target Cut 3.3% to A$4.35/Share by Ord Minnett
New Zealand, Canada to Resolve Dairy Dispute under CPTPP
data.description:
New Zealand and Canada agreed to resolve a dairy dispute under the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP), Trade and Investment Minister Todd McClay said in a media release Friday.

Canada has pledged to alter its dairy quota administration under the CPTPP, including reallocating underused quotas and imposing penalties for importers who misuse them, the release said.

New Zealand initiated dispute settlement proceedings against Canada over restrictive dairy export access under the CPTPP in 2022, according to the release.

data.title: Gold Road Resources Price Target Cut 2.7% to A$3.30/Share by Euroz Hartleys
US FDA Committee Advises Against Blenrep Combinations for Myeloma
data.description:
The US Food and Drug Administration's Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee voted against GSK's Blenrep combinations, citing concerns over the overall benefit/risk profile at the proposed dosage.

The pharmaceutical company said Thursday that it will continue working with the FDA as the US regulator concludes its Blenrep review, taking the committee's recommendation into account, before the July 23 Prescription Drug User Fee Act date.

The combinations of Blenrep, generically named belantamab mafodotin, were tested in adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who had received at least one prior line of therapy. Blenrep combinations are approved for the indication in the UK, Japan, Switzerland and other markets, with applications under review in the European Union and China.

data.title: Energy Transition Minerals Unit Secures Preferred Bidder Status for Spain Mine
data.description:
Energy Transition Minerals said its Spanish unit was recommended as the preferred bidder by a court-appointed administrator for the Penouta Mine in Spain, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

A final auction is set for July 29, with a court decision

expected by October, the filing said.

ETM's offer totals up to AU$3.6 million along with an AU$900,000 deposit.

The company may also cover up to AU$2.2 million in care and maintenance costs before closing, targeted by the end of the year.

data.title: Japan Inflation Eases, But BOJ Still Faces Policy Challenges — Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--A slight easing in Japan's consumer inflation is welcome news for the central bank, but stubbornly high food prices will be of concern for policymakers whose hands remain tied by tariffs.

Core consumer inflation, which excludes volatile fresh food prices, rose 3.3% from a year earlier in June, government data showed Friday. That compared with May's 3.7% rise, and matched the forecast in a poll of economists by data provider Quick.

The slowdown was due largely to cooler energy prices, which climbed 2.9%--much slower than the 8.1% increase seen in May as government gasoline subsidies kept price growth in check.

"Price pressures are cooling in Japan, which is very welcome," said Krishna Bhimavarapu, an economist at State Street Investment Management. "Even though we expect another hike from the Bank of Japan this year, our conviction is weakened."

While persistent inflation has been hurting consumer appetite, it may not immediately trigger an interest-rate increase by the central bank because of concerns about the impact of higher U.S. tariffs on the economy.

President Trump has threatened to impose a "reciprocal" tariff of 25% on Japanese exports starting on Aug. 1. Duties on autos--a core industry for Japan--are already in effect. Tokyo has been trying to negotiate a trade deal with Washington, but progress has been difficult.

The lack of clarity on tariffs complicates matters for the BOJ, which is also watching sticky price pressures, particularly in food.

Friday's data showed no sign of easing in food inflation in June, with rice prices still up around 100% on the year despite government efforts to bring down the cost of the staple by releasing reserves. Excluding fresh items, food prices increased 8.2%, compared with May's 7.7% rise.

That's unlikely to be of much comfort to the ruling coalition, which is facing a key electoral test this weekend.

Rising living costs are a central issue in the Upper House election scheduled for Sunday. Under pressure to maintain its majority, the ruling Liberal Democratic Party has promised a cash handout of 20,000 yen per person, or about $134. Opposition parties are calling for cuts to the consumption tax.

NLI Research Institute's Taro Saito expects core consumer inflation to fall below 3% in August due primarily to the slowdown in energy price growth and remain around the central bank's 2% target for the rest of the year.

"Businesses may be growing less resistant to price increases, after experiencing persistent inflation and the passing on of labor and logistics costs," Saito said, adding that food inflation in particular is expected to remain high for some time.

The BOJ's policy board is scheduled to release fresh price projections at its next meeting on July 30-31. Many economists say the central bank will likely keep policy settings steady again this month, even though it's not done tightening yet.

Judging solely by consumer price trends, the BOJ will likely be in a position to consider additional rate hikes at least through 2025, said Mizuho Securities economist Ryosuke Katagi.

As reaffirmed by the latest CPI data, domestic prices--especially for goods--remain strong, but with the conclusion of U.S.-Japan trade negotiations still uncertain, the BOJ will likely continue to cautiously assess the timing of interest-rate hikes, Katagi said.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Philippines posts $226 mln balance of payment surplus in June
data.description:
The Philippines' overall balance of payments (BOP) position was a surplus of $226 million in June after a deficit of $298 million in May, the central bank said on Friday.

The cumulative BOP level for the first six months was a deficit of $5.6 billion. The central bank has forecast a $6.3 billion deficit for 2025.

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Materials Sector Climbs, Leads All-Sector Rally
data.description:
The materials sector jumped almost 2% at midday Friday, leading a broad-based rally, as all sectors on the Australian Securities Exchange were trading in the green.

On Wall Street, both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite indices closed at record highs on Thursday, up 0.5% and 0.7%, respectively.

The S&P/ASX 200 index was up 1.1%.

BHP Group , which was up almost 3%, reported copper production of 516,200 tonnes in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, up 2% year-over-year and 1% from the prior quarter. Iron ore output was 70.3 million tonnes, up 2% from a year earlier and 14% from the fiscal third quarter.

In fiscal 2025, the company produced 2 million tonnes of copper and 263 million tonnes of iron ore, up 8% and 1%, respectively, from a year earlier.

data.title: BHP Group Logs Higher FY25 Production of Copper, Iron Ore; Steelmaking, Energy Coal Output Drops
data.description:
BHP Group (, BHG.JO) said Friday that production of copper and iron ore increased in fiscal 2025, while steelmaking coal, energy coal, and nickel output declined year over year.

For the 12 months ended June 30, copper production grew to 2,016,700 tonnes from 1,865,000 tonnes in the prior year, and iron ore output moved to 263 million tonnes from 260 million tonnes.

On the other hand, steelmaking coal production fell to 18 million tonnes from 22.3 million tonnes in the previous year, while energy coal output declined to 15 million tonnes from 15.4 million tonnes. Nickel production in Western Australia plunged to 30,200 tonnes from 81,600 tonnes a year ago.

data.title: Market Chatter: EQT, Omers, Phoenix Tower Mull Offers for Cellnex Telecom's Swiss Arm
data.description:
EQT , Omers Administration and Phoenix Tower are exploring final proposals for the acquisition of a majority stake in Cellnex Telecom's Swiss business, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Binding offers are expected to be submitted by the end of the week, the people said, noting that the business could be valued at up to 2 billion euros. The sources said deliberations between the parties continue, but the suitors may still decide against lodging a proposal.

Cellnex currently owns 72% of the business, with Swiss Life's Swiss Life Asset Managers holding the remaining interest.

Cellnex, EQT, Omers, and Phoenix did not immediately respond to requests for comments from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Lynas Rare Earths Price Target Cut 10% to A$7.80/Share by Ord Minnett
Lynas Rare Earths Downgraded to Sell from Hold by Ord Minnett
Sing Holdings, Sunway Developments Win Singapore Site in URA Tender; Shares Up Nearly 4%
data.description:
Sing Holdings , through its subsidiary, Sing Holdings Residential, and Sunway Developments, secured a land parcel on Chuan Grove in Singapore, awarded by the city-state's Urban Redevelopment Authority, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Shares of the company were up nearly 4% in recent trading.

The partners will now form a joint venture company to complete the acquisition and will undertake a residential development on the site.

data.title: Burgundy Diamond Mines Downgraded to Hold from Speculative Buy by Euroz Hartleys
Xiamen Tungsten's H1 Profit Slips 4%, Revenue Climbs 12%
data.description:
Xiamen Tungsten's net profit attributable to shareholders in the first half slipped 4.4% to 971.8 million yuan from 1.02 billion yuan a year earlier, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Friday.

Earnings per share dropped 15% year on year to 0.61 yuan from 0.72 yuan.

The Chinese tungsten carbide manufacturer's total operating revenue climbed 12% to 19.2 billion yuan from 17.2 billion yuan in the previous year, the disclosure said.

The company's shares rose less than 2% during morning trade.

data.title: Japan's Nikkei edges down, off near 2-week high amid election outcome worries
data.description:
Japan's Nikkei share average slipped from a more than two-week high to trade lower on Friday as investors weighed the outcome of the nation's upper house election due on the weekend.

The Nikkei slipped 0.31% to 39,778.85 by the midday break. Earlier, it had risen to as much as 40,087.59, its highest level since July 1, underpinned by the strong performance of Wall Street, but fell soon as investors started selling to book profits.

The S&P 500 stock index and the Nasdaq Composite both finished at record highs on Thursday, as investors embraced strong economic data and earnings reports that showed American consumers remained willing to spend.

For the week, the Nikkei is set to rise 0.5% and snap a two straight weeks of losses.

The broader Topix was down 0.13% to 2,836.1.

"Investors did not want to take a risk in buying stocks ahead of the national election on the weekend," said Yugo Tsuboi, chief strategist at Daiwa Securities.

"But the momentum is not bad as about half the stocks rose."

Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba's Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) and its partner Komeito are expected to lose their majority in the upper house on Sunday.

Strategists now focus on whether Ishiba will remain in his position or step down after the election, as a gauge of the nation's potential policy shift, which could lead to a cut in the national consumption tax.

Chip-related heavyweight fell, with Advantest and Tokyo Electron losing 4.27% and 0.7%, respectively.

Disco tanked 10.26% to become the biggest percentage loser on the Nikkei, as the chipmaking device supplier's quarterly operating profit forecast missed market expectations.

Uniqlo-brand owner Fast Retailing rose 0.79% to provide the biggest support to the Nikkei. Technology investor SoftBank Group rose 1.5%.

Of more than 1,600 stocks on the Tokyo Stock Exchange's prime section, 42% rose and 53% fell, and 4% traded flat.

data.title: Merrill Lynch (Singapore) Purchases 4.5 Million Shares in NTT DC REIT; Shares Down Nearly 1%
data.description:
Merrill Lynch (Singapore) purchased 4,451,600 shares in NTT DC REIT at a price range of 0.970 to 0.985 apiece, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Shares of the REIT were down nearly 1% in recent trading.

The purchase was made on July 17 as principal or through dealers on Merrill Lynch's behalf, the filing added.

data.title: Tiantan Biological Products Starts Phase Three Trial for IV Immunoglobulin
data.description:
Beijing Tiantan Biological Products subsidiary Chengdu Rongsheng Pharma has initiated a phase three trial for its intravenous immunoglobulin to treat chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy.

The trial follows ethics approval and pre-enrollment preparations, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Burgundy Diamond Mines Price Target Cut 52% to A$0.12/Share by Euroz Hartleys
Syntara Starts Phase 1b/2 Trial of Blood Cancer Treatment Candidate
data.description:
Syntara said a phase 1b/2 multi-center study evaluating its amsulostat drug candidate in combination with standard therapy 5-Azacitidine for the potential treatment of high-risk myelodysplastic neoplasms as well as chronic myelomonocytic leukemia was initiated with the University Medicine Mannheim in Germany as the first active study center, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The initial phase 1b trial will determine the safety profile and recommended dose of amsulostat alongside 5-Azacitidine, while the second phase component will further evaluate the safety and efficacy of the selected dose across 30 patients.

The trial will also seek to determine the candidate's ability to improve overall response as well as progression-free, event-free, and overall survival, per the filing. It will also evaluate the quality of life, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics.

Confirmed high-risk myelodysplastic neoplasms or chronic myelomonocytic leukemia patients who are either treatment-naïve or have undergone limited prior treatment with hypomethylating agents are eligible for the trial.

Nine planned centers are expected to enroll patients under the German MDS study group, the filing said.

The firm's shares rose almost 2% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Palm tracks rival Dalian oil higher, heads for third weekly gains
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures opened higher on Friday and headed for a third consecutive weekly gain, tracking the rise in Dalian palm oil contract.

The benchmark palm oil contract for October delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange gained 79 ringgit, or 1.88%, to 4,289 ringgit ($1,011.32) a metric ton, as of 0232 GMT.

The contract has gained 2.61% this week.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) rose 0.92%, while its palm oil contract increased 1.64%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade were up 0.34%.

* Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils, as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

* According to independent inspection company AmSpec Agri Malaysia, exports of Malaysian palm oil products for the July 1-15 period fell 5.3% from June 1-15, while cargo surveyor Intertek Testing Services reported a 6.2% drop.

* Meanwhile, Malaysia has hiked its August crude palm oil reference price, raising export duties to 9% from 8.5% in July.

* Indonesia's biodiesel consumption reached 7.42 million kilolitres this year, as of July 16, 47.5% of 2025's allocation.

* Indonesia's plantation fund agency estimates levies collected on palm oil will touch 30 trillion rupiah ($1.84 billion) this year, enough to finance the country's biodiesel mandate.

* Oil prices were little changed on Friday after rising in the previous session as concerns drone attacks on northern Iraqi oil fields will cut supply amid worries of potential demand declines amid uncertainty in U.S. tariff policy.

* Stronger crude oil futures make palm a more attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.

* The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, weakened slightly 0.02% against the dollar, making the commodity cheaper for holders of foreign currencies.

* Palm oil may retest resistance at 4,316 ringgit per metric ton, a break above which could lead to a gain into 4,354 ringgit to 4,392 ringgit range, Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said.

MARKET NEWS

Asian shares tracked Wall Street higher on Friday as still-strong U.S. economic data and robust corporate earnings offset tariff worries, while the yen headed toward a second successive week of loss ahead of Japan's upper house election.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

1230 US Housing Starts Number June

1400 US U Mich Sentiment Prelim July

($1 = 4.2410 ringgit)

data.title: Northern Dynasty: Motion for Summary Judgement Filed in Federal District Court in Alaska
data.description:
VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESS Newswire / July 17, 2025 / Northern Dynasty Minerals Ltd. (NYSE American:NAK) ("Northern Dynasty" or the "Company") and its 100%-owned U.S.-based subsidiary Pebble Limited Partnership ("Pebble Partnership") announce that, in the interest of ensuring timelines remain tight, we have filed a motion in Federal District Court in Alaska seeking a summary judgment briefing schedule in our pending litigation to get this question of the unlawfulness of the Biden veto before the court for prompt decision.

"While discussions with the EPA have taken place, we have not reached a settlement. As such, today we asked the court to set a briefing schedule for summary judgment motions, as we now believe that will be the quickest, most direct avenue to get the veto removed," said Ron Thiessen, Northern Dynasty President and CEO. "We will continue to work with the relevant government agencies to resolve this issue. Meanwhile we are confident that the court will agree with our assessment that the issuance of the veto by the Biden administration was unlawful."

Mr. Thiessen continued, "This administration has been emphatic about its desire for the U.S. to be self-sufficient in critical metals like copper and to be the global AI capital. For this to happen, the U.S. must develop secure domestic supplies of important metals such as copper and the withdrawal of this egregious and unsubstantiated veto of the largest undeveloped copper project in the world would go a long way towards achieving this goal."

About Northern Dynasty Minerals Ltd.

Northern Dynasty is a mineral exploration and development company based in Vancouver, Canada. Northern Dynasty's principal asset, owned through its wholly owned Alaska-based U.S. subsidiary, Pebble Limited Partnership, is a 100% interest in a contiguous block of 1,840 mineral claims in Southwest Alaska, including the Pebble deposit, located 200 miles from Anchorage and 125 miles from Bristol Bay. The Pebble Partnership is the proponent of the Pebble Project.

For further details on Northern Dynasty and the Pebble Project, please visit the Company's website at www.northerndynastyminerals.com or contact Investor services at (604) 684-6365 or within North America at 1-800-667-2114. Public filings, which include forward looking information cautionary language and risk factor disclosure regarding the Company and the Pebble Project can be found in Canada at www.sedarplus.ca and in the United States at www.sec.gov.

Ronald W. Thiessen

President & CEO

U.S. Media Contact:

Dan Gagnier, Gagnier Communications (646) 569-5897

Forward Looking Information and other Cautionary Factors

This document includes certain statements that may be deemed "forward-looking statements" under the United States Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 and under applicable provisions of Canadian provincial securities laws. All statements in this document, other than statements of historical facts, which address permitting, including the withdrawal of the EPA veto and the development and production for the Pebble Project are forward-looking statements. Additional forward looking statements made by the Company under its continuous disclosure obligations include statements regarding (i) the development plan for the Pebble Project (ii) the right-sizing and de-risking of the Pebble Project, (iii) the design and operating parameters for the Pebble Project development plan, including projected capital and operating costs, (iv) the social integration of the Pebble Project into the Bristol Bay region and benefits for Alaska, (v) the political and public support for the permitting process, (vi) the ability of the Pebble Project to ultimately secure all required federal and state permits, (vii) the ability of the Company and/or the State of Alaska to challenge the Environmental Protection Agency's ("EPA's") Final Determination process under the Clean Water Act and ultimately the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers Record of Decision ("USACE ROD") through legal actions; (viii) exploration potential of the Pebble Project, (ix) future demand for copper, gold and other metals, (x) if permitting is ultimately secured, the ability to demonstrate the Pebble Project is ultimately commercially viable, and (xi) the potential addition of partners in the Pebble Project. Although NDM believes the expectations expressed in these forward-looking statements are based on reasonable assumptions, such statements should not be in any way be construed as guarantees that the Pebble Project will secure all required government permits or regarding the ability of NDM to develop the Pebble Project in light of the USACE ROD and its subsequent remand decision and the EPA's Final Determination, establish the commercial feasibility of the Pebble Project, achieve the required financing or develop the Pebble Project.

Forward-looking statements are necessarily based upon a number of factors and assumptions that, while considered reasonable by NDM as of the date of such statements, are inherently subject to significant business, economic and competitive uncertainties and contingencies. Assumptions used by NDM to develop forward-looking statements include the assumptions that (i) the Pebble Project will obtain all required environmental and other permits and all land use and other licenses without undue delay, (ii) any feasibility studies prepared for the development of the Pebble Project will be positive, (iii) NDM's estimates of mineral resources will not change, and NDM will be successful in converting mineral resources to mineral reserves, (iv) NDM will be able to establish the commercial feasibility of the Pebble Project, and (v) NDM will be successful in its legal action against the EPA and the USACE and any action taken by the EPA in connection with the Final Determination will ultimately not be successful in restricting or prohibiting development of the Pebble Project.

In addition, the likelihood of future mining at the Pebble Project is subject to a large number of risks and will require achievement of a number of technical, economic and legal objectives, including (i) the current development plan may not reflect the ultimate mine plan for the Pebble Project, (ii) obtaining necessary mining and construction permits, licenses and approvals without undue delay, including without delay due to third party opposition or changes in government policies, (iii) finalization of the mine plan for the Pebble Project, (iv) the completion of feasibility studies demonstrating that any Pebble Project mineral resources that can be economically mined, (v) completion of all necessary engineering for mining and processing facilities, (vi) the ability of NDM to secure a partner for the development of the Pebble Project, and (vi) receipt by NDM of significant additional financing to fund these objectives as well as funding mine construction. NDM is also subject to the specific risks inherent in the mining business as well as general economic and business conditions. Investors should also consider the risk factors identified in the Company's Annual Information Form for the year ended December 31, 2024, as filed on SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca) and included in its annual report on Form 40-F filed on EDGAR (www.sec.gov), as well as the risk factors set out in the Company's subsequent public continuous disclosure filings available on SEDAR+ and EDGAR. For more information on the Company, Investors should review the Company's filings with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission at www.sec.gov and its home jurisdiction filings that are available at www.sedarplus.ca.

The National Environment Policy Act Environmental Impact Statement process requires a comprehensive "alternatives assessment" be undertaken to consider a broad range of development alternatives, the final project design and operating parameters for the Pebble Project and associated infrastructure may vary significantly from that currently contemplated. As a result, the Company will continue to consider various development options and no final project design has been selected at this time.

SOURCE: Northern Dynasty Minerals Ltd.

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: China Hanking Secures FIRB Approval for Australia Gold Unit Spin-Off
data.description:
China Hanking said it has received approval from the Australian Foreign Investment Review Board (FIRB) for restructuring plans related to the proposed spin-off and listing of its Australian gold unit, Hanking Gold, according to a Thursday bourse filing.

Shares of the company were down about 1% in recent Friday trade.

The restructuring is part of a broader plan to list Hanking Gold on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange by way of introduction.

Hanking Gold holds two key projects in Australia, the Cygnet project in Western Australia and the Mt Bundy project in the Northern Territory, and aims to establish itself as a mid-tier gold producer.

data.title: Sun Silver Reports 26% Higher Silver Grades in Re-Assay Program; Shares Up 7%
data.description:
Sun Silver reported up to 26% higher silver grades after analyzing preliminary silver results from partial pulp re-assays at its Maverick Springs project in Nevada, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The historical pulp re-assay program, performed using a four-acid digest method, showed consistent uplift in mean silver grades across samples from the initial five holes tested, the filing said.

Up to 195 holes drilled before 2024 are available for reassessment based on the reassay program outcomes, per the filing.

Shares rose nearly 7% in midday trade Friday.

data.title: Singapore's REITs Expected to Remain Resilient to Trade Uncertainty — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's real estate investment trusts look attractively valued, Morningstar analysts say in a report. They note sell-offs in industrial and logistics REITs in 2Q amid concerns U.S. tariffs could disrupt regional supply chains. Still, "Singapore REITs proved resilient during the previous trade war, and we expect them to remain robust through the current one as well," they say.Morningstar's top picks are Keppel REIT and Frasers Logistics & Commercial Trust, which offer appealing dividend yields of 6.4% and 7.1%, respectively. Keppel REIT's office portfolio and healthy balance sheet should enable it to weather economic uncertainty. FLCT's logistics and industrial assets should benefit from healthy leasing demand and strong occupancy. Morningstar has fair value estimates of S$1.16 on Keppel REIT and S$1.08 on FLCT. They are at S$0.90, and S$0.85, respectively. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Medical device maker Microport loses most in two weeks on tepid first-half outlook
data.description:
** Shares of orthopaedic and cardiovascular medical devices manufacturer Microport Scientific Corp fall 6.1% to HK$8.66, their biggest intraday pct drop since July 4

** Company expects to record up to $110 million loss in H1, compared with $106.7 million loss last year, with revenue down about 4% y/y

** Says results hit by volatile geopolitical and international trade conflicts, and intensified domestic industry competition, among others

** Except the surgical robot business, all of the other major business segments recorded revenue decline or growth slowdown to varying extents - Microport

** Hong Kong's healthcare index (.HSCIH) adds 0.1% and Hang Seng Index climbs 0.9%

** YTD, stock up 35.7%
